contents:
# Vietnam protests China's illegal land reclamation at Antelope Reef in Paracel Islands

## Overview
Hanoi objected to Beijing's dredging and landfill activities at Antelope Reef, asserting sovereignty over Hoang Sa archipelago. Vietnam's Ministry of Foreign Affairs spokesperson called the activities completely illegal and invalid. China claims the Paracels as Xisha territory with no dispute, stating works improve island living conditions.[1]

## Details
Activities observed via satellite imagery by Asia Maritime Initiative at Antelope Reef (Lingyang Jiao in Chinese, Hai Sam in Vietnamese). If continued at current rate, it could become largest feature in Paracels and entire South China Sea, rivaling Mischief Reef. Paracel Islands located 260 miles from Vietnam coast, 186 miles southeast of China. China maintains 20 outposts in Paracels. Protest led by spokesperson Pham Thu Hang last month. Hanoi claims sufficient evidence and legal grounds for sovereignty.[1]

**Location**: Antelope Reef, Paracel Islands, South China Sea

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @XHNews, which covers Xinhua news China official, PLA exercises, Taiwan, South China Sea. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Xinhua news China official will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
